description: An observer disposed from inside a callback is NOT invoked by the notification in flight, including when the loop has not yet reached it (#lzdartobservercow). This is the clause the family actually disagreed on. Cell `topic` covers the not-yet-visited case: `obs_b` unsubscribes `obs_d` on its first run, and `obs_d` — three positions behind the cursor — MUST NOT run in that pass. Cell `back` covers the already-visited case and the symmetric direction: `obs_q` unsubscribes `obs_p`, which has already run, and that invocation MUST stand, because disposal is not retroactive; `obs_p` is simply absent from every later pass. Cell `self` covers an observer unsubscribing itself mid-callback, which MUST complete the call it is in and never run again. What breaks otherwise, and why the majority position lost: a stable pre-notification snapshot (lazily-dart `95acb1d`, lazily-go `8781f2b`) invokes a disposed observer one final time in that pass. Under a tracing collector that is harmless — the snapshot keeps the closure alive. In a manually-managed binding, `unsubscribe` is routinely the step immediately before freeing the state the callback reads, so 'one more call after you asked to stop' is a use-after-free with a specification behind it. A contract safe in five bindings and unsound in three is the wrong family default. The mechanism is free: tombstone-and-skip (lazily-zig `9d72b14`), a liveness check before each call, or a re-check of the registration all conform. What a binding MUST NOT do is relocate an unvisited observer behind the notify cursor — a swap-remove under a live iteration silently drops whichever entry it moved, which is the defect this section was written from, and the `obs_e` tail assertions below are what catch it. Assertions are on `observed_order` only. Known to fail today against lazily-dart and lazily-go.

expect.note: obs_d is skipped in the pass that removed it; obs_e — the tail — still runs, which is the swap-remove regression this pins
the removal is permanent, and the survivors keep their relative order
already-visited: obs_p ran before it was disposed and that invocation stands — disposal is not retroactive
self-unsubscribe completes the call it is in — the callback must not be aborted — and obs_u, behind the cursor, is unaffected
